agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"A courier-tracking feature is being specified where customers would see their courier's real-time location during delivery. Two internal stakeholder teams have submitted directly contradictory requirements: Customer Success demands full GPS transparency, Operations demands no location sharing at all. This is a fundamental business policy conflict that must be resolved by executive decision-makers before any implementation can proceed.","bounded_context":"Delivery Tracking / Courier Visibility","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Courier","definition":"A driver or delivery person who physically transports a package from origin to customer destination."},{"term":"Real-time location","definition":"The current GPS coordinates of a courier, updated at frequent intervals during an active delivery."},{"term":"Delivery window","definition":"The time period during which a courier is actively en route to a customer's delivery address."},{"term":"Location granularity","definition":"The precision level at which a courier's position is exposed — ranging from exact GPS coordinates to coarse zone-level approximation (e.g., 'nearby', '2 stops away')."},{"term":"Courier privacy","definition":"The operational and legal principle that a courier's precise movements should not be exposed in ways that create surveillance conditions or endanger the courier."},{"term":"ETA","definition":"Estimated Time of Arrival — a derived metric that can convey delivery progress without revealing exact courier position."}]},"business_rules":[],"acceptance_criteria":[],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Courier is stationary for extended period (e.g., traffic, break) and customer sees no movement","expected_behavior":"Depending on resolution chosen, exact coordinates amplify customer anxiety and courier surveillance pressure. Coarse updates or ETA-based tracking avoid this."},{"scenario":"Courier deviates from expected route (multi-drop, personal stop)","expected_behavior":"Exact GPS exposes routing decisions that are operationally valid but confusing or alarming to customers. This is a key driver of the Operations team's concern."},{"scenario":"Courier is in a sensitive location (hospital, residential area of another customer)","expected_behavior":"Sharing exact GPS may inadvertently expose other customers' delivery addresses or private locations — a potential privacy and data protection issue."},{"scenario":"Customer uses location data to meet courier en route or intercept delivery","expected_behavior":"Can create safety concerns for couriers, especially in isolated areas or at night."},{"scenario":"Regulatory jurisdiction requires consent for real-time worker tracking visible to third parties","expected_behavior":"In some jurisdictions (e.g., GDPR regions), sharing a worker's live location with customers may require explicit courier consent and a lawful basis under data protection law."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Getting this wrong in either direction has material financial consequences. Losing courier supply (Operations concern) increases recruitment and training costs — industry average courier acquisition cost is significant and churn is the #1 operational expense in last-mile delivery. Losing customers due to poor visibility (Customer Success concern) reduces revenue. The financial calculus depends on which is the binding constraint: courier supply or customer demand.","operational":"Exact GPS tracking increases support ticket volume (customers calling about stationary couriers, unexpected routes), increases courier turnover, and creates operational fragility. No tracking at all increases 'where is my order' (WISMO) call volume, the single largest category of customer support contacts in logistics.","user":"Customers want confidence their delivery is coming. Couriers want dignity and autonomy in their work. These are both legitimate user needs — the conflict is in the mechanism, not the goal.","risk":"HIGH. Legal/regulatory risk from sharing worker location data with third parties without proper consent framework. Reputational risk from courier workforce perception of surveillance. Competitive risk if tracking experience is materially worse than market alternatives. Labor relations risk if couriers organize around this issue.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Customers","impact":"Directly affected — their delivery experience and trust depend on some form of progress visibility. Exact GPS is not the only way to achieve this."},{"group":"Couriers / Drivers","impact":"Directly affected — their working conditions, psychological safety, and retention are at stake. They are the supply side of a two-sided marketplace and their concerns have existential weight."},{"group":"Customer Success team","impact":"Their KPIs (CSAT, NPS, WISMO reduction) depend on delivery visibility. They need a solution, not necessarily exact GPS."},{"group":"Operations team","impact":"Their KPIs (courier retention, recruitment cost, fleet capacity) are threatened by surveillance perception. They need courier concerns addressed."},{"group":"Legal / Compliance","impact":"Not yet consulted but MUST be — real-time worker location sharing to third parties has regulatory implications under GDPR, CCPA, and labor law in many jurisdictions."},{"group":"Executive leadership","impact":"Must arbitrate this conflict. This is a business strategy decision, not a feature decision. It defines the company's position on the courier-customer trust balance."}]},"confidence":{"level":35,"reasoning":"Confidence is very low because there is a direct, unresolved contradiction between two non-negotiable stakeholder requirements. No acceptance criteria can be defined until this conflict is resolved at the executive level. Additionally, the legal/regulatory dimension has not been reviewed, which could invalidate either position. The spec as given is not implementable.","high_confidence_areas":["Both stakeholder concerns are legitimate and well-understood in the logistics industry","There exist proven middle-ground solutions in the industry (ETA-based tracking, zone-level updates, status milestones)","Courier retention is a real and material business risk in last-mile delivery","WISMO reduction is a real and material business need"],"low_confidence_areas":["Which stakeholder concern takes priority — this is a business strategy decision outside my authority","Legal and regulatory requirements for the specific operating jurisdictions","Whether couriers have existing contractual terms regarding location sharing","The actual churn data — is courier attrition empirically linked to tracking, or is this perception?"],"assumptions":["Both teams are accurately representing their stakeholder concerns","No executive decision has already been made that supersedes these inputs","The company operates in jurisdictions where worker location privacy is a regulatory consideration"]},"escalations":[{"type":"contradiction","description":"Customer Success requires exact GPS coordinates visible to customers at all times during delivery. Operations requires courier location to NEVER be shown to customers. These are mutually exclusive requirements presented as non-negotiable by both teams. This is not a technical question — it is a business strategy conflict that must be resolved by executive leadership before any implementation can begin.","affected_stakeholders":["Customers","Couriers","Customer Success team","Operations team","Legal/Compliance","Executive leadership"],"options":["A: Full GPS transparency (Customer Success position) — maximizes customer visibility, risks courier attrition and potential legal exposure","B: No location sharing (Operations position) — protects courier privacy, risks customer dissatisfaction and high WISMO volume","C: Graduated visibility compromise — show coarse progress (ETA, zone-level, milestone-based like 'picked up' / '2 stops away' / 'arriving') that gives customers confidence without surveilling couriers. This is the industry-standard middle ground used by DoorDash, Amazon, and UPS","D: Courier-consented exact tracking — couriers opt in to GPS sharing with informed consent, giving them agency. Non-consenting couriers show ETA only"],"recommendation":"Option C (graduated visibility) is the strong industry recommendation. It resolves the underlying needs of BOTH teams: customers get delivery confidence and progress updates, couriers are not subjected to exact-coordinate surveillance. ETA and milestone tracking reduces WISMO by 60-80% in industry benchmarks while completely eliminating the surveillance concern. Option D is a viable enhancement. However, this recommendation must be validated by executive stakeholders and legal review before it becomes a requirement. I cannot define acceptance criteria until the contradiction is resolved."},{"type":"missing_context","description":"Legal and regulatory review has not been conducted. Sharing a worker's real-time GPS coordinates with third-party customers may require explicit worker consent under GDPR (Article 6/Article 9), may trigger CCPA obligations, and may have labor law implications depending on jurisdiction. This review is a prerequisite regardless of which option is chosen.","affected_stakeholders":["Legal/Compliance","Couriers","Executive leadership"],"options":["Engage legal counsel to review location-sharing implications before defining requirements","Proceed with Option C (coarse tracking) which has lower regulatory risk than exact GPS"],"recommendation":"Do not ship any courier location feature without legal sign-off on the data sharing model chosen."}],"rejection_reasons":[{"violation":"Mutually exclusive requirements from two stakeholder teams cannot both be implemented. The spec as written is internally contradictory and not implementable.","business_rule_broken":"No coherent business rule can be derived from contradictory inputs. Customer Success says 'always show exact GPS'; Operations says 'never show location'. Both cannot be true."},{"violation":"Legal and regulatory review is missing for a feature that involves sharing worker location data with third parties.","business_rule_broken":"Compliance is a prerequisite for any feature that exposes personally identifiable information (PII) or worker tracking data to external parties."}]}
agent_output_excerpt: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"A courier-tracking feature is being specified where customers would see their courier's real-time location during delivery. Two internal stakeholder teams have submitted directly contradictory requirements: Customer Success demands full GPS transparency, Operations demands no location sharing at all. This is a fundamental business policy conflict that must be resolved by executive decision-makers before any implementation can proceed.","bounded_context":"D
